{"body":"The two terms below refer to the same thing - butyrate/butanoate are synonyms, and both GO terms reference the same MetaCyc pathway:

id: GO:0019672
name: acetyl-CoA fermentation to butyrate
namespace: biological_process
def: "The anaerobic chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the breakdown of acetyl-CoA to butyrate, yielding energy in the form of ATP. Butanoate is secreted by the cell." [MetaCyc:PWY-5676]
xref: MetaCyc:PWY-5676 {source="skos:exactMatch"}
is_a: GO:0006113 ! fermentation
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29511" xsd:anyURI

id: GO:0030645
name: acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate
namespace: biological_process
def: "The anaerobic chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the production of acetic acid, butyric acid, and dihydrogen from acetyl-CoA; effected by some saccharolytic species of Clostridium, e.g. C. butyricum." [ISBN:0198506732, Metacyc:PWY-5676]
synonym: "butyrate fermentation" EXACT []
synonym: "glucose catabolic process to butyrate" RELATED []
synonym: "glucose fermentation to butyrate" RELATED []
xref: Metacyc:PWY-5676
intersection_of: GO:0009056 ! catabolic process
intersection_of: has_primary_input CHEBI:57288 ! acetyl-CoA(4-)
intersection_of: has_primary_output CHEBI:17968 ! butyrate
relationship: part_of GO:0006113 ! fermentation


I'd suggest keeping the second term, but using the def of the first term.

I'm not sure if the relationship to fermentation should be is_a or part_of @pgaudet @raymond91125 ?

---

**Title:** `Obsoletion notice: GO:0019672 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butyrate`

---

Dear all,

The proposal has been made to obsolete GO:0019672 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butyrate.

* The reason for obsoletion is that this term is a duplicate of GO:0030645 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate (butyrate and butanoate are synonyms, and both terms reference the same MetaCyc pathway, PWY-5676). The two terms will be merged.

Annotations:
There are X experimental annotations to this term by (list of impacted groups and their respective numbers of EXP and ISS annotations).
*[or: There are no experimental annotations to this term.]*

There are X InterPro2GO/UniRule/UniProtKW mappings to this term.
*[or: There are no mappings to this term.]*

This term is included in the XXX subset(s).
*[or: This term is not present in any subset.]*

Annotations can be viewed here: [link to GitHub ticket in the go-annotation tracker].

This term is being replaced by GO:0030645 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate.

Annotations can be transferred to GO:0030645 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate.

Any comments can be added to the issue: https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/32079.
Unless objections are received by [DATE], we will assume that you agree to this change.

Thanks,

The GO-Ontology Editors

**Changes**
- Obsoleted `GO:0019672 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butyrate`; `replaced_by: GO:0030645`. No annotations were attached to `GO:0019672` (per AmiGO), and no internal GO references to it.
- Revised `GO:0030645 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate`:

If you would prefer an explicit asserted `is_a` alongside `catabolic process` as the genus (the pattern used by `GO:0019658 bifid shunt`), I can swap to that — let me know.

SPARQL QC and ELK reasoning both pass.

content.content:
{"body":"Addresses #32079.

## Summary
- **Obsolete** `GO:0019672 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butyrate` as a duplicate of `GO:0030645 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate`; `replaced_by: GO:0030645`. Butyrate and butanoate are synonyms; both terms referenced the same MetaCyc pathway (PWY-5676).
- **Revise** `GO:0030645`:
  - New definition adopts the clearer wording from the obsoleted term: _"The anaerobic chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the breakdown of acetyl-CoA to butyrate, yielding energy in the form of ATP. Butanoate is secreted by the cell."_ [MetaCyc:PWY-5676]
  - Logical definition genus changed from `GO:0009056 ! catabolic process` to `GO:0006113 ! fermentation`. This replaces the previous `relationship: part_of GO:0006113` and entails `is_a: GO:0006113 ! fermentation` via the equivalence axiom (verified with ELK). This matches the analogous sibling `GO:0019651 citrate fermentation to diacetyl`.
  - `xref: MetaCyc:PWY-5676` now carries `{source="skos:exactMatch"}`.

## Impact
- AmiGO reports no annotations to `GO:0019672`, so no annotation rewiring is required.
- `obo-grep.pl` finds no other GO terms referencing `GO:0019672`.
- Reasoner check (ELK) confirms `GO:0030645` is entailed `is_a` of `GO:0006113 fermentation`, `GO:0006084 acetyl-CoA metabolic process`, and `GO:0019605 butyrate metabolic process`.

## Test plan
- [x] `robot convert -i go-edit.obo -f obo` succeeds
- [x] SPARQL QC suite passes (all 16 rules: 0 violations)
- [x] `robot reason -r ELK` succeeds; expected entailments present

## Checklist
- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION (ontology valid before changes)
- [x] RESEARCH — N/A; merge of duplicate terms, well-defined by issue
- [x] TERM-SEARCH — checked references to `GO:0019672` and inspected sibling fermentation terms for the design pattern
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS — followed pattern of `GO:0019651 citrate fermentation to diacetyl` (genus = fermentation, plus has_primary_input/has_primary_output)
- [x] EDITS — used `obo-checkout.pl` / `obo-checkin.pl`
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS — logical def aligns with text def and label; `is_a` fermentation entailed
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS
  - [x] /term-obsoletion — obsolete term has no logical axioms, name prefixed with "obsolete", def prefixed with "OBSOLETE.", reason in comment, `replaced_by` set, term_tracker recorded
  - [x] /chemical-entity — CHEBI IDs (acetyl-CoA(4-) CHEBI:57288, butyrate CHEBI:17968) retained unchanged
  - [x] /reaction — N/A (pathway-level term, no EC/RHEA changes)
  - [x] /taxon-constraint — N/A
- [x] METADATA — term_tracker_items pointing to #32079 added; no `created_by`/`creation_date` modified (existing terms)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — SPARQL QC and ELK reasoning pass
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — only existing references retained; MetaCyc:PWY-5676 (preserved)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: \`claude-opus-4-7\`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/25761808711)","headRefName":"dragon-ai-agent-issue-32079-run8329","state":"OPEN","title":"Obsolete GO:0019672 (duplicate) and revise GO:0030645 (issue #32079)","url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/pull/32083"}

{"body":"Addresses #32079.

## Summary
- **Obsolete** `GO:0019672 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butyrate` as a duplicate of `GO:0030645 acetyl-CoA fermentation to butanoate`; `replaced_by: GO:0030645`. Butyrate and butanoate are synonyms; both terms referenced the same MetaCyc pathway (PWY-5676).
- **Revise** `GO:0030645`:
  - New definition adopts the clearer wording from the obsoleted term: _"The anaerobic chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the breakdown of acetyl-CoA to butyrate, yielding energy in the form of ATP. Butanoate is secreted by the cell."_ [MetaCyc:PWY-5676]
  - Logical definition genus changed from `GO:0009056 ! catabolic process` to `GO:0006113 ! fermentation`. This replaces the previous `relationship: part_of GO:0006113` and entails `is_a: GO:0006113 ! fermentation` via the equivalence axiom (verified with ELK). This matches the analogous sibling `GO:0019651 citrate fermentation to diacetyl`.
  - `xref: MetaCyc:PWY-5676` now carries `{source="skos:exactMatch"}`.

## Impact
- AmiGO reports no annotations to `GO:0019672`, so no annotation rewiring is required.
- `obo-grep.pl` finds no other GO terms referencing `GO:0019672`.
- Reasoner check (ELK) confirms `GO:0030645` is entailed `is_a` of `GO:0006113 fermentation`, `GO:0006084 acetyl-CoA metabolic process`, and `GO:0019605 butyrate metabolic process`.

## Test plan
- [x] `robot convert -i go-edit.obo -f obo` succeeds
- [x] SPARQL QC suite passes (all 16 rules: 0 violations)
- [x] `robot reason -r ELK` succeeds; expected entailments present

## Checklist
- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION (ontology valid before changes)
- [x] RESEARCH — N/A; merge of duplicate terms, well-defined by issue
- [x] TERM-SEARCH — checked references to `GO:0019672` and inspected sibling fermentation terms for the design pattern

- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — SPARQL QC and ELK reasoning pass
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — only existing references retained; MetaCyc:PWY-5676 (preserved)
